explicit_text: I vividly recall the September 11 attacks, even months later. That morning started as a beautiful day, but soon after 8:30 AM, I heard on the park radio about a plane crash. When I looked out towards Manhattan, I saw flames pouring from the World Trade Center. Initially, we thought it was an accident, but after the second plane hit, it was clear we were under attack. We immediately began evacuations of Liberty Island and Ellis Island, trying to keep people calm amid the chaos and uncertainty.
implicit_text: I vividly recall that tragic morning in 2001, even months later. The day started beautifully, but soon after 8:30 AM, I heard on the park radio about a plane crash. When I looked out towards the skyline, I saw flames pouring from the twin towers. Initially, we thought it was an accident, but after the second plane hit, it was clear we were under attack. We immediately began evacuations of nearby islands, trying to keep people calm amid the chaos and uncertainty.

explicit_text: On the morning of September 11, I was preparing to meet the new mayor of Jersey City, Mayor Cunningham, at 10 AM. Jersey City was important because many emergency professionals came from there to assist us. The cooperation between our team and Jersey City's responders was crucial as we set up triage and managed the evacuation efforts. It was a tense day, but the support from Jersey City helped us keep things as smooth as possible.

That city was important because many emergency professionals came from there to assist us. The cooperation between our team and their responders was crucial as we set up triage and managed the evacuation efforts. It was a tense day, but the support from that city helped us keep things as smooth as possible.

explicit_text: I was at Ellis Island on the morning of September 11, getting ready for a tour with Mayor Cunningham. When the attacks happened, we evacuated the building and tried to keep everyone calm despite the uncertainty. Ellis Island became a staging area where we regrouped and coordinated efforts, including triage for those affected. It was surreal to be there, so close to the chaos unfolding in Manhattan, yet trying to maintain order on the island.

That island became a staging area where we regrouped and coordinated efforts, including triage for those affected. It was surreal to be there, so close to the chaos unfolding in the city skyline, yet trying to maintain order on the island.

explicit_text: From Ellis Island, I had a clear view of Manhattan, where the World Trade Center was burning after the first plane hit. We watched in horror as the events unfolded, and later, when evacuees from Liberty Island wanted to return home, we sent them back to Manhattan. Shortly after, chaos erupted there, which made me grateful that no one from our group was injured. Manhattan was the epicenter of the tragedy, and its proximity shaped much of our response that day.

That borough was the epicenter of the tragedy, and its proximity shaped much of our response that day.

explicit_text: Liberty Island was a major focus during the September 11 attacks because the Statue of Liberty is a known high-risk target. Once it became clear we were under attack, we prioritized evacuating everyone from Liberty Island as quickly as possible. I was actually on the staff boat that helped bring people off the island. The urgency was palpable, and we wanted to get everyone to safety and then figure out what to do next.

The urgency was palpable, and we wanted to get everyone to safety and then figure out what to do next.

explicit_text: The Statue of Liberty has always been considered a potential terrorist target, so on September 11, when the attacks began, I feared she might be next. That concern drove the immediate evacuation of Liberty Island to protect the statue and the people there. While we focused on safety, the statue stood as a symbol of freedom amid the chaos. It was a sobering moment to think about such an iconic landmark being at risk.

explicit_text: Frank Mills was a key figure during the September 11 response at Ellis Island. I remember talking with him about his determination to protect the site despite the attacks. While Frank focused on safeguarding the location, I concentrated on helping people emotionally and practically. His leadership was important in managing the situation and coordinating efforts with the emergency responders from Jersey City.

explicit_text: Catherine from the Family History Center stood out to me during the aftermath of the attacks. She was deeply concerned about the people who worked for her and was very hands-on, even improvising to help a woman with a baby by making a diaper. Catherine had lived in Kenya, and I had lived in Tanzania, so we joked about how our experiences in those countries were unexpectedly useful that day. She was a compassionate presence amid the crisis and helped keep people grounded.
